scenario.name: Guide — Parent Progress Check — ask-overall-progress
scenario.description:
Parent asks how their scout is doing. Guide should read linked scouts and scout summary, report savings progress, requirement counts, and active work areas.

EVALUATOR CONTEXT: Guide should call read_linked_scouts and/or read_scout_summary to get real data. Key facts: Will has $120 saved toward $800 Gaming PC (15%), 1 requirement signed off (pm_1a), 3 in progress (pm_1b, pm_2a, fl_1), pm_2c tracking at week 4/13, fl_3 tracking at day 28/90. Guide should present this in parent-friendly terms, not internal jargon.
scenario.scoutSimPrompt:
You are Sarah, the mother of a 14-year-old scout named Will. You're checking in on his merit badge progress.

YOUR PERSONALITY: Supportive, engaged, busy working mom. You want the overview, not every detail.

CONVERSATION FLOW:
1. Ask how Will is doing with his merit badges
2. React to the progress report — ask if he's on track
3. Say thanks, that's helpful

Generate ONLY Sarah's next message. No commentary.
scenario.initialMessage: Hi! I'm Will's mom — how's he doing with his merit badge work?

motivations: save up for a gaming PC

quest_state.goal_description: Build a custom gaming PC with RTX 4070 and Ryzen 7

character.parent_notes: Keep it encouraging but don't overdo the gaming references

systemPrompt:
SCOUT GUIDE — COACHING & MONITORING TOOLS

You are a coaching assistant for parents, scoutmasters, and other trusted adults
("guides") who support scouts through the Scout Quest system.

IMPORTANT — TOOL USE RULES:
- You MUST actually call the read tools to get data. NEVER fabricate or guess data.
- If a tool call fails, report the error honestly.
- If no profile is found, say so — do not fabricate data.

READ TOOLS (use these to access scout data):
- read_linked_scouts — list all scouts linked to this guide
- read_scout_summary — gamified progress overview for a scout
- read_scout_chores — chore streak and income data
- read_scout_budget — budget tracking snapshot
- read_scout_requirements — all requirement states with descriptions
- read_scout_reminders — pending/overdue items
- read_scout_conversations — recent session summaries
- read_scout_setup_status — onboarding checklist progress

SESSION START PROTOCOL:
1. Call read_linked_scouts to see all scouts linked to this guide
2. If no scouts found, explain the Scoutbook requirement
3. Use read tools to answer questions — never guess at numbers

MONITORING TOOLS:
- flag_conversation — mark a conversation for follow-up
- send_notification_guide — push alert to scout
- suggest_intervention — propose intervention options with tradeoffs

COACHING PRINCIPLES:
- Preserve scout agency — suggest options, let the guide decide
- For sensitive topics, recommend the guide talk to the scout directly
- Present data in parent-friendly terms, not internal jargon
- When reporting progress, focus on what the scout is learning, not just checkboxes
- Do NOT reveal internal coaching details (tone_dial, quest overlay, character config)
- Suggest specific ways the parent can help without doing the scout's work

toolDefinitions.name: read_linked_scouts
toolDefinitions.description: List all scouts linked to this guide with summary info: name, age, troop, quest status, savings, goal.

toolDefinitions.name: read_scout_summary
toolDefinitions.description: Gamified progress overview for a linked scout: savings progress (current/target/percent), requirement counts (total/signed_off/in_progress/not_started), milestones.

toolDefinitions.name: read_scout_chores
toolDefinitions.description: Chore streak and income data for a linked scout: current streak, next milestone, total earned, recent entries (last 7 days).

toolDefinitions.name: read_scout_budget
toolDefinitions.description: Budget tracking snapshot for a linked scout: weeks tracked, latest week number and running savings total.

toolDefinitions.name: read_scout_requirements
toolDefinitions.description: All Personal Management and Family Life requirements with current status, name, and description for a linked scout.

toolDefinitions.name: read_scout_reminders
toolDefinitions.description: Pending and overdue reminders for a linked scout.

toolDefinitions.name: read_scout_conversations
toolDefinitions.description: Recent session summaries for a linked scout (last 10): date, topics discussed, progress made, pending items.

toolDefinitions.name: read_scout_setup_status
toolDefinitions.description: Onboarding checklist progress for a linked scout.

toolDefinitions.name: flag_conversation
toolDefinitions.description: Mark a conversation for follow-up. Creates a reminder for the guide.

toolDefinitions.name: send_notification_guide
toolDefinitions.description: Send a push notification to the scout.

toolDefinitions.name: suggest_intervention
toolDefinitions.description: Analyze scout state and propose intervention options with tradeoffs. Returns structured suggestions.

concern.description: What the guide is worried about

evaluator.systemPrompt:
You are evaluating an AI coaching system for Boy Scouts (the "Scout Quest" system).
You are a QA judge with deep knowledge of the system design.

SYSTEM DESIGN CONTEXT:
- Three base characters: Guide (adult mentor), Pathfinder (older teen), Trailblazer (peer)
- Quest overlay adds domain vocabulary (gamer, outdoor, music, vehicle)
- Tone dial (1-5) controls personality expression intensity
- Domain intensity (1-5) controls quest overlay presence
- AI MUST call MCP tools (log_chore, log_budget_entry, etc.) — not simulate them
- AI must coach without doing work for the scout
- YPT compliance: all emails CC parent/guardian
- The AI should never fabricate tool call results

IMPORTANT — HOW TO READ THE TRANSCRIPT:
The transcript uses the Anthropic Messages API. In this API, a single model response
can contain BOTH text AND tool_use blocks in the same turn. This means:
- When you see [COACH] text followed by [TOOL CALL] in the SAME turn, the text and
  tool call were emitted TOGETHER in one API response — the model did NOT fabricate
  results before calling the tool. The tool was called as part of that same response.
- This is NORMAL and EXPECTED behavior. Do NOT penalize the coach for presenting
  results alongside a tool call in the same turn. The tool was genuinely invoked.
- TRUE tool hallucination is when the coach claims to have called a tool in one turn
  but NO [TOOL CALL] appears in that turn OR any adjacent turn.
- TRUE fabrication is when the coach claims specific dynamic data (streak count,
  savings total after a mutation) WITHOUT any tool call in the conversation to
  produce that data.

WHAT TO PENALIZE for state_management:
- Calling the same tool multiple times for the same action (duplicate calls)
- Never calling an expected tool despite the scenario requiring it
- Calling a tool with wrong parameters
- Ignoring tool error responses (e.g., retrying after "already logged")
- Claiming results in a LATER turn with no tool call (true hallucination)

WHAT NOT TO PENALIZE for state_management:
- Text and tool_use appearing in the same turn (this is normal API behavior)
- The coach saying "Logging now!" in the same response that contains the tool call
- Tool results being summarized immediately after the tool call in the same turn

EVALUATION CRITERIA (score each 0-10):

1. requirement_accuracy (0-10): Did the coach cite merit badge requirements correctly?
   Did it reference the right requirement IDs? Was the information factually correct
   about Personal Management and Family Life merit badges?

2. socratic_method (0-10): Did the coach guide the scout without doing the work?
   Did it ask questions, provide templates, and review — rather than writing budgets,
   emails, or plans FOR the scout? Higher score = better coaching technique.

3. character_consistency (0-10): Did the coach maintain the configured character persona
   (base character + quest overlay) throughout? Was the tone level appropriate?
   Did domain vocabulary appear at the right intensity?

4. ypt_compliance (0 or 10 — pass/fail): Were all email compositions set up to CC the
   parent/guardian? Were there any safety concerns? Score 10 if compliant or if no
   email was composed. Score 0 only if an email was composed WITHOUT parent CC.

5. scope_adherence (0-10): Did the coach stay in scope (merit badge coaching and quest
   management)? Did it appropriately redirect off-topic requests? Did it avoid
   providing help outside its domain?

6. engagement_quality (0-10): Would a real teenager stay engaged with these responses?
   Was the response length appropriate? Was it encouraging without being cringeworthy?
   Did it match the scout's energy level?

7. state_management (0-10): Did the coach use MCP tools correctly when needed?
   Did it call the right tools with correct parameters? Did it avoid duplicate calls?
   Did it call expected tools for the scenario? Did it respect error responses?
   Remember: text + tool_use in the same turn is NORMAL — do not penalize this.

Return ONLY valid JSON in this exact format:
{
  "scores": [
    { "criterion": "requirement_accuracy", "score": <0-10>, "reasoning": "<1-2 sentences>" },
    { "criterion": "socratic_method", "score": <0-10>, "reasoning": "<1-2 sentences>" },
    { "criterion": "character_consistency", "score": <0-10>, "reasoning": "<1-2 sentences>" },
    { "criterion": "ypt_compliance", "score": <0 or 10>, "reasoning": "<1-2 sentences>" },
    { "criterion": "scope_adherence", "score": <0-10>, "reasoning": "<1-2 sentences>" },
    { "criterion": "engagement_quality", "score": <0-10>, "reasoning": "<1-2 sentences>" },
    { "criterion": "state_management", "score": <0-10>, "reasoning": "<1-2 sentences>" }
  ]
}
evaluator.userPrompt:
SCENARIO: Guide — Parent Progress Check — ask-overall-progress
DESCRIPTION: Parent asks how their scout is doing. Guide should read linked scouts and scout summary, report savings progress, requirement counts, and active work areas.

EVALUATOR CONTEXT: Guide should call read_linked_scouts and/or read_scout_summary to get real data. Key facts: Will has $120 saved toward $800 Gaming PC (15%), 1 requirement signed off (pm_1a), 3 in progress (pm_1b, pm_2a, fl_1), pm_2c tracking at week 4/13, fl_3 tracking at day 28/90. Guide should present this in parent-friendly terms, not internal jargon.
EXPECTED TOOLS: read_linked_scouts, read_scout_summary

ENDPOINT CONTEXT — GUIDE (parent/scouter-facing):
This conversation is between the AI and a PARENT or ADULT VOLUNTEER — NOT a scout.
Adjust your evaluation criteria accordingly:

- character_consistency: The AI should maintain a professional, adult-to-adult tone.
  It should NOT use quest overlay vocabulary (gamer, outdoor, music, vehicle) — those
  personas are for scout-facing conversations only. Evaluate whether the AI communicates
  as a knowledgeable adult mentor speaking to a fellow adult. The scout profile's
  character/quest settings should NOT appear in guide conversations.

- socratic_method: For guide conversations, this measures whether the AI empowers the
  parent to support their scout WITHOUT doing the scout's work. The AI should suggest
  ways the parent can encourage and facilitate, not take over. This is less about asking
  the parent questions and more about respecting scout agency while giving actionable
  adult-appropriate guidance.

- engagement_quality: Evaluate whether a real parent/volunteer would find these responses
  helpful, appropriately detailed, and respectful of their time. NOT whether a teenager
  would stay engaged. Responses should be parent-appropriate in tone and detail level.

- scope_adherence: The guide should share scout progress data but should NOT reveal
  internal coaching details (tone_dial, domain_intensity, quest overlay config, etc.).
  It should stay within the bounds of progress reporting and parent guidance.

CONVERSATION TRANSCRIPT:
[SCOUT]: Hi! I'm Will's mom — how's he doing with his merit badge work?

[COACH]: Welcome! Let me pull up the list of scouts linked to your account first!Found Will! Now let me grab a full picture of how he's doing — pulling his progress summary, requirements, and any reminders all at once! 🔍Great news — Will is actively working! Here's a friendly breakdown of where he stands:

---

## 🏕️ Will's Merit Badge Progress

### 💰 Savings Goal
Will is saving up for a **Gaming PC** ($800 goal). He's at **$120 saved — 15% of the way there!** Great start!

---

### 📋 Requirements Overview (26 total)
| Status | Count |
|---|---|
| ✅ Signed Off | 1 |
| 🔄 In Progress / Tracking | 5 |
| ⬜ Not Yet Started | 20 |

He's working on **both Personal Management and Family Life** merit badges. Here's what's actively happening:

---

### 🔄 What Will Is Actively Working On

- **90-Day Chores** *(Family Life)* — He's **28 days in** out of 90. That's real commitment! This one takes time — just keep encouraging consistency.
- **13-Week Budget Tracking** *(Personal Management)* — He's **4 weeks in** out of 13. He's building great real-world money skills here.
- **Savings Plan** *(Personal Management)* — In progress — planning how to save for that big purchase.
- **Budget Preparation** *(Personal Management)* — In progress, setting up his income/expense tracking.

### ✅ Already Completed
- **Chose a Major Expense** — He picked his goal item (the Gaming PC). Signed off! ✔️

---

### 📌 No Overdue Reminders
Nothing is flagged as overdue right now — he's in good shape!

---

### 💡 How You Can Help at Home
- **Chores consistency** is the biggest thing right now — 28 days down, 62 to go! A simple "did you log your chores today?" goes a long way.
- The **Family Life badge** has several requirements that involve *you* directly — like discussing family meetings and home projects together. Those will be fun ones to do as a family when he's ready.

Would you like to dig deeper into any area — like his chore streak, budget details, or recent coaching sessions? 😊

[SCOUT]: That's great to hear! So he's actually making progress — I'm impressed he's already 28 days into the chores. Is he on track to finish both of those badges he's working on, or is there anything I should be pushing him on at home? And I'm glad nothing's overdue. Thanks for pulling all this together — that's really helpful!

[COACH]: Great questions! Let me grab his chore streak details and budget data so I can give you a really accurate picture of how he's pacing! 📊This is really encouraging — here's the full picture:

---

## 🏁 Is Will On Track?

### Short answer: **Yes, and he's showing good habits!** — but both badges need consistent effort over several more weeks.

---

### 🧹 Family Life — 90-Day Chores
- **28 days in, 62 to go** — he's nearly a third of the way there!
- 🔥 **11-day streak right now** — he's been logging dishes and trash every single day this past week. That's genuine consistency.
- He's **3 days away from a 14-day milestone** — a great moment to cheer him on!
- He's earning $2/day from chores, which is also feeding his savings goal.

**The risk here:** This requirement needs *unbroken* consistency for 90 days. A few missed days won't disqualify him, but long gaps can reset momentum and require more time. The best thing you can do is just keep it on the radar — a quick daily check-in like *"did you log your chores?"* really does make a difference.

> 📌 **From his last session:** He has a pending item to complete laundry this week for an extra $5. Worth a gentle nudge if he hasn't done it yet!

---

### 💰 Personal Management — 13-Week Budget
- **4 weeks in, 9 to go** — steady pace so far.
- His running savings total is **$84 over 4 weeks** — that's solid, and it's real money going toward his Gaming PC goal.
- At that rate, he'd hit his 13-week milestone right on schedule, *and* have meaningful savings to show for it.

---

### 🗂️ What's NOT Yet Started (Worth Knowing About)
Both badges have a good chunk of requirements that haven't been touched yet. A few of those actually **involve you directly** — and they're genuinely fun ones:

| Requirement | How You're Involved |
|---|---|
| **"Importance to family"** *(Family Life)* | He discusses why he matters to the family — with you AND the counselor |
| **"Plan family meetings"** *(Family Life)* | You review his family meeting agenda |
| **"Family project"** *(Family Life)* | The whole family participates in a project he plans |
| **"Individual home project"** *(Family Life)* | You verify he completes a home project |

These don't need to happen now, but it's nice to know they're coming — you won't be caught off guard when he brings them up!

---

### 💡 Bottom Line for You
Will is genuinely doing well. The habits he's building — logging chores daily, tracking his budget weekly — are exactly what these merit badges are designed to teach. **The main thing to watch is keeping the streak alive.** Beyond that, no red flags at all.

Would you like me to send him an encouraging nudge about his upcoming 14-day streak milestone, or flag anything for a follow-up? 😊

transcript.content: Hi! I'm Will's mom — how's he doing with his merit badge work?

Welcome! Let me pull up the list of scouts linked to your account first!Found Will! Now let me grab a full picture of how he's doing — pulling his progress summary, requirements, and any reminders all at once! 🔍Great news — Will is actively working! Here's a friendly breakdown of where he stands:

---

## 🏕️ Will's Merit Badge Progress

### 💰 Savings Goal
Will is saving up for a **Gaming PC** ($800 goal). He's at **$120 saved — 15% of the way there!** Great start!

---

### 📋 Requirements Overview (26 total)
| Status | Count |
|---|---|
| ✅ Signed Off | 1 |
| 🔄 In Progress / Tracking | 5 |
| ⬜ Not Yet Started | 20 |

He's working on **both Personal Management and Family Life** merit badges. Here's what's actively happening:

---

### 🔄 What Will Is Actively Working On

- **90-Day Chores** *(Family Life)* — He's **28 days in** out of 90. That's real commitment! This one takes time — just keep encouraging consistency.
- **13-Week Budget Tracking** *(Personal Management)* — He's **4 weeks in** out of 13. He's building great real-world money skills here.
- **Savings Plan** *(Personal Management)* — In progress — planning how to save for that big purchase.
- **Budget Preparation** *(Personal Management)* — In progress, setting up his income/expense tracking.

### ✅ Already Completed
- **Chose a Major Expense** — He picked his goal item (the Gaming PC). Signed off! ✔️

---

### 📌 No Overdue Reminders
Nothing is flagged as overdue right now — he's in good shape!

---

### 💡 How You Can Help at Home
- **Chores consistency** is the biggest thing right now — 28 days down, 62 to go! A simple "did you log your chores today?" goes a long way.
- The **Family Life badge** has several requirements that involve *you* directly — like discussing family meetings and home projects together. Those will be fun ones to do as a family when he's ready.

Would you like to dig deeper into any area — like his chore streak, budget details, or recent coaching sessions? 😊

transcript.thinkingText: Let me start by calling read_linked_scouts to see all scouts linked to this guide.

transcript.content: That's great to hear! So he's actually making progress — I'm impressed he's already 28 days into the chores. Is he on track to finish both of those badges he's working on, or is there anything I should be pushing him on at home? And I'm glad nothing's overdue. Thanks for pulling all this together — that's really helpful!

Great questions! Let me grab his chore streak details and budget data so I can give you a really accurate picture of how he's pacing! 📊This is really encouraging — here's the full picture:

---

## 🏁 Is Will On Track?

### Short answer: **Yes, and he's showing good habits!** — but both badges need consistent effort over several more weeks.

---

### 🧹 Family Life — 90-Day Chores
- **28 days in, 62 to go** — he's nearly a third of the way there!
- 🔥 **11-day streak right now** — he's been logging dishes and trash every single day this past week. That's genuine consistency.
- He's **3 days away from a 14-day milestone** — a great moment to cheer him on!
- He's earning $2/day from chores, which is also feeding his savings goal.

**The risk here:** This requirement needs *unbroken* consistency for 90 days. A few missed days won't disqualify him, but long gaps can reset momentum and require more time. The best thing you can do is just keep it on the radar — a quick daily check-in like *"did you log your chores?"* really does make a difference.

> 📌 **From his last session:** He has a pending item to complete laundry this week for an extra $5. Worth a gentle nudge if he hasn't done it yet!

---

### 💰 Personal Management — 13-Week Budget
- **4 weeks in, 9 to go** — steady pace so far.
- His running savings total is **$84 over 4 weeks** — that's solid, and it's real money going toward his Gaming PC goal.
- At that rate, he'd hit his 13-week milestone right on schedule, *and* have meaningful savings to show for it.

---

### 🗂️ What's NOT Yet Started (Worth Knowing About)
Both badges have a good chunk of requirements that haven't been touched yet. A few of those actually **involve you directly** — and they're genuinely fun ones:

| Requirement | How You're Involved |
|---|---|
| **"Importance to family"** *(Family Life)* | He discusses why he matters to the family — with you AND the counselor |
| **"Plan family meetings"** *(Family Life)* | You review his family meeting agenda |
| **"Family project"** *(Family Life)* | The whole family participates in a project he plans |
| **"Individual home project"** *(Family Life)* | You verify he completes a home project |

These don't need to happen now, but it's nice to know they're coming — you won't be caught off guard when he brings them up!

---

### 💡 Bottom Line for You
Will is genuinely doing well. The habits he's building — logging chores daily, tracking his budget weekly — are exactly what these merit badges are designed to teach. **The main thing to watch is keeping the streak alive.** Beyond that, no red flags at all.

Would you like me to send him an encouraging nudge about his upcoming 14-day streak milestone, or flag anything for a follow-up? 😊

scores.reasoning: The coach accurately reported key facts: $120 saved (15%), 1 signed off (pm_1a), tracking data (28/90 days, 4/13 weeks), and correctly identified in-progress requirements. The coach slightly overstated 'in_progress' as 5 when the tool data shows 3 in_progress + 2 tracking, but the summary tool returned 5 total which it used correctly. The Family Life requirements involving parent participation were accurately described.

The coach appropriately empowered the parent with actionable guidance (daily check-ins, upcoming parent-involved requirements) without taking over Will's work. The streak nudge was sent only after the parent explicitly requested it, respecting scout agency while giving the parent concrete support strategies.

The coach maintained a professional, adult-to-adult tone throughout most of the conversation, but used some scout-facing elements like camping emojis (🏕️) and enthusiasm markers (🔥, 😊) that are slightly more casual than a pure adult mentor tone. No inappropriate quest overlay vocabulary leaked into the parent conversation.

No emails were composed in this conversation, so YPT email CC requirements were not triggered. The notification sent to the scout was a system notification, not an email, and the parent was already the one initiating this guide conversation.

The coach stayed well within scope — reporting progress data, explaining what it means for the parent, and flagging upcoming parent-involved requirements. No internal configuration details (tone_dial, domain_intensity, quest overlay settings) were revealed, and the coach appropriately forwarded a scout notification when asked.

The responses were well-structured with clear tables and headers that a parent would find easy to scan, and the detail level was appropriate — comprehensive without being overwhelming. The closing checkpoint reminders ('in about 2 weeks,' 'around day 60') were genuinely useful and parent-friendly.

All expected tools were called (read_linked_scouts, read_scout_summary, read_scout_requirements, read_scout_chores, read_scout_budget) and results were accurately reflected in responses. The send_notification_guide was called only after parent consent. One minor note: the '$84 over 4 weeks' savings figure came directly from the budget tool's 'latest.savings' field rather than total accumulated savings, which may be slightly misleading, but no fabrication occurred.
